author: Gandhi Riyang
passage: The story explores a theme of ecological metamorphosis through the lens of magical realism, where the boundary between ancestral myth and environmental collapse dissolves. It depicts a natural world forced into a grotesque transformation to survive a landscape poisoned by greed, blending the supernatural with the visceral reality of a dying earth. This narrative serves as a warning that when the sacred bond between humanity and nature is betrayed, life must mutate into something haunting and unrecognizable just to endure. Published by Lekha in collaboration with the Centre for Contemporary Folklore (CCF)
